mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in monthly retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand and strengthens corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month change on 20-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls to or below 0.5%, it indicates an extremely accommodative monetary stance that compresses discount rates and supports asset prices, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
regime_hint: zero lower bound / ultra-loose monetary regime
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p>0.10) when trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: Joint behavior of low unemployment rate (below 5%) and rising nonfarm payrolls month-over-month signals a tight labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: tight labor market (UNRATE < 5% and PAYEMS MoM positive)
failure_condition: A long-short portfolio formed on composite z-score of UNRATE and PAYEMS shows no significant spread over 20-day horizon in walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust industrial production (index value above 100), continued strength in INDPRO signals healthy economic expansion that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon. In weak regimes, the signal reverses.
failure_condition: When INDPRO is above 100, Rank IC of its monthly change on 60-day returns is negative or insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the CPI inflation rate above 3% year-over-year triggers market expectations of monetary tightening, which re-rates equity valuations downward, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Average 5-day forward return following CPIAUCSL YoY > 3% is not significantly negative at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: When both housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) rise above their respective 3-month moving averages, it signals synchronized demand strength that boosts equity market sentiment and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Long-short portfolio on the joint z-score shows insignificant cumulative returns over any 2-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate above 6% with month-over-month acceleration signals labour market deterioration that erodes consumer confidence and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Average 60-day forward return following trigger is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05) or more than 40% of trigger instances produce positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: During episodes of strong GDP growth (quarterly annualized above 3%), positive GDP surprises lead to upward revisions in earnings expectations and immediate positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon. In weak growth regimes, the same surprise has no effect.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive following GDP surprises in high-growth regimes, or the effect is not distinguishable from baseline periods.
